commit.message:
Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Claude API with local PDF parser + keyword classifier

- Remove anthropic dependency entirely
- Add pdfplumber for PDF text extraction
- Keyword-based transaction classifier (8 spending categories, 100+ merchants)
- Detects grocery stores, gas stations, telecoms, Costco/Walmart/Amazon frequency
- No API key required, zero external cost

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix watchlist not updating immediately after adding from search

wlAddFromSearch was only updating #wl-list if it existed — failed when
list was empty (no #wl-list in DOM). Now just calls renderWatchlist().

zellij: disable tips on session attach

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Migrate audio backend from PyAudio to sounddevice

sounddevice provides cleaner error handling on Linux/ALSA, ships pre-built
wheels (no C compilation needed), and integrates natively with NumPy float32
arrays, eliminating the int24 byte-packing step from every audio callback.

Changes in music/synth_engine.py:
- Replace pyaudio import with sounddevice (sd)
- list_output_devices(): rewritten using sd.query_devices() / sd.query_hostapis()
  (Windows WASAPI deduplication preserved)
- Stream setup: sd.OutputStream replaces pyaudio.PyAudio().open()
- _audio_callback: signature updated (outdata, frame_count, time, status);
  silence paths fill outdata array instead of returning bytes;
  final output writes float32 directly into outdata[:,0/1] — int24 packing removed
- close(): stream.stop() / stream.close() replaces stop_stream / terminate
- Pre-allocated int32 output buffer (_cb_out) removed (no longer needed)

Other changes:
- pyproject.toml, requirements.txt: pyaudio replaced with sounddevice>=0.4.6
- run.sh: system dep section updated — installs PortAudio runtime library only
  (libportaudio2 / portaudio), no compiler or -devel headers required
- README.md: all PyAudio references updated to sounddevice; troubleshooting
  and platform setup sections updated with correct package names

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: transform sample-mode merge guard + Beneish component winsorization (session 51)

Phase A: transform.py `--sample N` now merges new rows into existing
company_financials.parquet instead of overwriting (KI-026 variant). New rows
take precedence on (corp_code, year) conflicts. Full runs rebuild from scratch.

Phase B: beneish_screen.py adds inf→NaN replacement + per-year 1%/99%
component winsorization (Beneish 1999 methodology). All 8 components now
finite. BENEISH_EXTREME_OUTLIERS constant deleted from src/constants.py
(59 entries — was defined and tested but never imported by any analysis script).

Metrics: 7,447 rows, flagged 1,255→1,250, 0 inf in m_score (was 111).
Tests: 235 pass (223 invariants + 12 acceptance).

fix: resolve critical type and behavioral inconsistencies across packages (#340)

## Summary

- Consolidated duplicated GraphQL types across `core`, `codegen`, and
`common` packages into single source of truth
- Unified `isGqlDefinitionCall` semantics across `builder`, `tsc`,
`babel`, and `formatter` to support `FunctionExpression` and
`TSNonNullExpression` unwrapping
- Fixed error handling in builder evaluation to throw on transpilation
failures instead of silently skipping
- Added defensive null check for operation type resolution in tagged
template composer
- Resolved schema, plugin, and CLI inconsistencies identified through
codebase investigation

## Changes

- **Type consolidation**: Moved shared types (`SchemaIndex`,
`ObjectRecord`, etc.) to `@soda-gql/core`
- **AST detection**: All 4 packages now consistently recognize `gql!`
(non-null assert) and function expressions
- **Error handling**: Builder now properly surfaces transpilation errors
with typed `BuilderError`
- **Plugin interfaces**: Added `errors` field to
`SwcTransformerInterface` return type
- **Documentation**: Fixed inconsistencies in READMEs and guides

## Test plan

- [x] All 2328 tests pass (0 failures)
- [x] TypeScript type check passes
- [ ] Manual verification with sample projects using various schema
configurations

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

commit.message:
fix: use consistent table formatting in `xv history` (#94)

`xv history` was using raw `Table::new().to_string()` instead of the
shared `format_table()` utility, resulting in plain unstyled output.
Now uses the same rounded borders, centered blue headers, and padding
as `xv ls` and `xv share list`.

Bumps version to 0.4.17.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(stt): switch utterance STT to faster-whisper for Australian English support

Whisper was trained on diverse global English data and handles AU/UK/IE
accents significantly better than the US-English sherpa-onnx Zipformer.

Changes:
- Add load_whisper_model() + transcribe_whisper() using faster-whisper-base.en
- Add _do_transcribe() helper: whisper > sherpa-onnx > vosk priority chain
- Auto-detect snapshot dir under models/whisper/models--Systran--faster-whisper-base.en/
- Only load sherpa-onnx if whisper is unavailable (saves ~1s startup)
- Log active STT backend on startup
- run-wake: expose PX_WHISPER_MODEL env var; pass --whisper-model to px-wake-listen
- transcribe: language="en", vad_filter=True, beam_size=3 for speed+accuracy

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add visible spiral vortex effect on spin (R key)

Spin is the #2 most-used interaction (50 uses). This adds animated
spiral arms that bloom from center, rotate, and fade when R is pressed,
making the most popular action much more visually rewarding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix duplicate title spans and improve garbled Hindi detection

- group_into_lines: skip duplicate span text within the same y-group —
  some PDFs render the title multiple times as a design layer, causing
  the same text to be repeated 5x in the output
- extract_title: also deduplicate cluster lines as a second safety net
- _looks_garbled: detect mixed-case-within-words pattern (e.g. 'LoLFk',
  'dSls', 'fgUnh') which is a hallmark of legacy Hindi font encoding
  but was previously not caught by symbol-based regex patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Presage.jsx API_BASE pointing to frontend port instead of backend

API_BASE was hardcoded to http://localhost:5176 (the Vite dev server port),
causing every POST /api/incidents, /api/triage, /api/schedule, and
/api/healthcard request to hit the frontend and return 404.

Changed to: import.meta.env.VITE_API_BASE || "http://localhost:3001"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Make auto-route note context-aware based on greeting audio

When the active schedule's greeting has no audio file, the note now
reads: "No Off Hours greeting audio — caller transfers immediately
to 10003-Main-Closed. Keys below are not reachable."

When there IS audio: "Caller hears the Off Hours greeting, then
transfers to 10003-Main-Closed. Keys below are only available
during playback."

commit.message:
fix(persistence): correct patient persistence — eliminate data loss on refresh

Root causes fixed:
1. Anti-pattern: removed setState calls inside another setState updater
2. Cloud sync was overwriting localStorage with profiles missing patients field
   → now merges: cloud.patients ?? localPatients before writing localStorage
3. Replaced inline persistence with useEffect watching patients changes
4. skipPatientsPersist ref prevents spurious cloud saves during hydration
5. patientsRef always tracks latest patients for use in closures

commit.message:
feat: observability, context wiring, tool ordering, and test coverage

Explore stage observability
- Emit agent_text reasoning note before each tool_call event so the UI
  activity feed shows intent → action rather than bare tool names
- Add submit_result reminder to the user turn so the model has the
  instruction in its most-attended context (prevents plain-text endings)
- _describe_tool_call() helper synthesizes human-readable notes per tool

Provider fixes (ClaudeCLI)
- Remove output_format from ClaudeAgentOptions: setting it lets the SDK
  return structured output from training knowledge, skipping tool calls
- submit_result now fires the on_tool_call callback so the UI registers
  the final submission event (was invisible to the frontend before)

Tool ordering & descriptions (codebase_tools)
- get_repo_summary moved to index 0; models call tools listed first
- list_repo_files description no longer says "Call this first",
  eliminating the conflicting priority that caused models to skip
  get_repo_summary entirely

Pipeline context wiring
- file_contexts, url_fetched_contexts, and instructions now forwarded to
  run_optimize (both initial call and retry); previously only run_analyze
  received them, leaving the optimizer without user-supplied context

Frontend: full TraceView integration
- Replace inline event-list stub with TraceView component
- ForgeRecord extended with changes_made, optimization_notes, issues,
  verdict, and per-stage model fields
- Stage results hydrated with model name and full output fields when
  loading from history

Gitignore: exclude .venv_*/ backup venvs
- .venv_py310_backup/ and similar backups no longer tracked

Tests (+513 lines across 2 files)
- P9: ClaudeCLIProvider must not pass output_format to ClaudeAgentOptions
- T-submit-1: submit_result fires on_tool_call callback
- T-model-1: MODEL_ROUTING['explore'] must be claude-haiku-4-5
- T-tools-1: list_repo_files must not claim 'Call this first' priority
- T-tools-2: get_repo_summary must be first in build_codebase_tools list
- T-reasoning-1/2/3: _describe_tool_call per-tool output validation
- T-reasoning-4: agent_text event emitted before tool_call
- T-explore-1: user turn must mention submit_result
- P-prop-1: run_pipeline forwards file/url/instruction contexts to optimizer

commit.message:
fix: recording audio corruption, multi-channel pipeline, and waveform flickering (#313)

* fix: recording audio corruption and waveform flickering

Three fixes:

1. AudioWorklet buffer overflow at non-48kHz sample rates — the 128-sample
   quantum doesn't divide evenly into the buffer (e.g., 705 at 44100Hz),
   causing ~9% sample loss and distorted audio. Now loops to handle frame
   boundary crossings.

2. useAudioTracks uses real AudioContext.sampleRate instead of hardcoded
   48000 fallback when building tracks.

3. Channel.tsx uses useLayoutEffect instead of useEffect for canvas drawing,
   preventing clearRect from being visible for one frame (caused flickering
   on load and during recording).

Also derives recording preview durationSamples from peaks data instead of
timer, keeping clip container and waveform content widths in sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix formatting in TonePlayout test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: update CLAUDE.md files with recording and canvas drawing patterns

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: guard against infinite loop when bufferSize is zero

If process() runs before the 'start' command configures bufferSize,
the while loop would never advance. Add early return guard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use useLayoutEffect for all channel canvas drawing

PianoRollChannel and SpectrogramChannel now use useLayoutEffect
consistently with Channel.tsx. Layout thrashing is not a concern
because ClipContainer and channel Wrapper set explicit dimensions
via .attrs() before canvases mount.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: record all channels instead of only first channel

The worklet was capturing all channels into separate buffers but
flushBuffers() only sent buffers[0]. Now sends all channel data
to the main thread, accumulates per-channel, and creates a
multi-channel AudioBuffer on stop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: multi-channel live waveform preview during recording

Recording peaks are now per-channel arrays instead of a single
mono array. PlaylistVisualization renders a ChannelWithProgress
for each channel during the live recording preview, and track
height accounts for the recording channel count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: make peak bit depth configurable via RecordingOptions.bits

Previously hardcoded as `const bits: 8 | 16 = 16`, now exposed
as an option (default 16) so consumers can use 8-bit peaks for
lower memory usage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: set channelCount on AudioWorkletNode for stereo recording

The AudioWorkletNode was created without channelCount/channelCountMode,
so the browser downmixed stereo mic input to mono before samples
reached the worklet. Now passes channelCount with 'explicit' mode.

RecordingExample updated to request 2 channels.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: show both channels after stereo recording stops

The RecordingExample had `mono` on the provider, which merged stereo to
mono in the peak generation pipeline after recording stopped — while
the live preview bypassed this and showed both channels.

Also made the live recording preview respect the `mono` flag for
consistency: when mono is set, only render the first channel during
recording (matching post-recording behavior).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: auto-detect microphone channel count from stream

Instead of hardcoding channelCount, read the actual channel count from
the MediaStream track settings via getSettings().channelCount. This
ensures mono mics record 1 channel and stereo mics record 2 channels
without manual configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review findings — zoom bug, race condition, error handling

- Fix durationSamples using zoom-dependent samplesPerPixel (revert to
  duration * sampleRate which is zoom-independent)
- Fix race condition: reset state refs before connecting source and
  posting start command to worklet
- Fix mono flag not applied in height pre-computation loop
- Add channels[0] guard in onmessage handler
- Add console.warn to empty catch blocks per project convention
- Add warning when stream channel auto-detection falls back
- Flow bits through recordingState instead of hardcoding 16
- Extract getTrackChannelCount() helper (DRY — was duplicated 3x)
- Extract emptyPeaks() module-level helper
- Update CLAUDE.md docs for recording and browser packages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review findings

- Update llm-reference.md with correct multi-channel types (peaks,
  bits, channelCount)
- Make createAudioBuffer backwards compatible (accepts single
  Float32Array or Float32Array[])
- Compute samplesProcessedBefore before incrementing totalSamplesRef
  for clarity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Scaffold Phase 0 monorepo with full test suite (263 tests)

Establish the complete Nuit One monorepo infrastructure:

- Turborepo + pnpm workspaces with 6 packages (apps/web, apps/api,
  packages/shared, packages/db, packages/ui, packages/audio-engine)
- SvelteKit 5 app with Tailwind v4, COOP/COEP headers for
  SharedArrayBuffer, Janua OAuth auth hooks, R2 storage client,
  AudioWorklet bridge, and file-based routing
- Hono API service with Janua JWT validation middleware (jose/JWKS)
- Drizzle ORM schema (projects, tracks, stems, performances,
  calibration_profiles) referencing Janua identity UUIDs
- "Nuit Glass" design system: Neon-Noir tokens (colors, typography,
  spacing, effects, animations) and Svelte 5 components (GlassCard,
  Button, NeonBadge)
- C/C++ WASM audio engine scaffold with SPSC ring buffer,
  passthrough processor, Emscripten CMake config, and TS bindings
- Shared types package covering audio, MIDI, project, workspace,
  performance, and calibration domains
- Enclii deployment configs, Soketi k8s manifest, Cloudflare Tunnel
  routing
- Architecture docs and phased roadmap (Phases 0-7)
- Vitest configured across all 6 packages with 263 passing tests

commit.message:
fix: chunk heatmap batch download to prevent timeouts (#372)

* fix: chunk heatmap batch download to prevent timeouts

yf.download with ~500 S&P 500 tickers regularly exceeded the 15s timeout.
Split into concurrent chunks of 50 via asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True)
so partial chunk failure only loses that chunk's tickers, not the whole batch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use BaseException for gather type narrowing and fix formatting

Mypy requires isinstance(result, BaseException) (not Exception) to narrow
the union from asyncio.gather(return_exceptions=True). Also fixes ruff
formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Refactorizar aplicando principio DRY

Cambios para eliminar duplicación de código y datos:

- Crear config/contacts.js como fuente única de verdad para contactos
- Crear componente ContactDropdown reutilizable para el dropdown
- Eliminar duplicación de datos de contactos (antes en 2 lugares)
- Eliminar duplicación de JSX del dropdown (antes en 2 componentes)
- Usar variantes (default/brand) para estilos según contexto
- Reducir ~40 líneas de código duplicado

Beneficios:
- Single Source of Truth: contactos definidos una sola vez
- DRY: componente reutilizable en lugar de código repetido
- Mantenibilidad: cambiar contactos solo requiere editar 1 archivo
- Consistencia: el dropdown funciona igual en ambos contextos

## Summary
- Replace `as any` with `as { id: number } | null` in list slug page for
Supabase relation join type
- Replace `as any` with `as unknown as SupabaseClient` in history
repository tests

causing CI failure

introduced when the files were created but violated the project's strict
lint rules.

remain in unrelated file)

commit.message:
feat(admin-auth): login page, route guard, and routeTree registration (#189)

* feat(admin-auth): backend foundation — DB migration, role/permission model, admin config

- V2 Flyway migration: admin_users, admin_sessions, admin_mfa_backup_codes tables
  with brute-force lockout fields (failed_attempts, locked_until) and MFA scaffold
- AdminRole enum (ADMIN, OPERATOR, FINANCE, AUDITOR, HELPDESK)
- AdminPermissions: 37 atomic permissions with role→permission matrix + check() / requirePermission()
- AdminModels: AdminLoginRequest/Response, AdminUserResponse, AdminCreate/UpdateUserRequest
- AppConfig: adminJwtSecret (HS256), adminJwtIssuer, adminAccessTokenTtlMs, adminRefreshTokenTtlDays
- build.gradle.kts: add at.favre.lib:bcrypt:0.10.2 for admin password hashing

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin-auth): backend auth service, routes, and DI wiring

- AdminAuthService: bcrypt password verify (at.favre.lib:bcrypt), brute-force lockout
  (5 attempts → 15-min lock), single-use refresh token rotation via SHA-256 hash in DB,
  JWT HS256 issuance/verification, admin user CRUD
- AdminAuthRoutes: POST /admin/auth/{login,refresh,logout}, GET /admin/auth/me,
  GET/POST/PATCH /admin/users, DELETE /admin/users/{id}/sessions
  — httpOnly SameSite=Strict cookies; refresh cookie narrowed to /admin/auth path
- Authentication.kt: added admin-jwt-hs256 named config alongside existing jwt-rs256
- Routing.kt: mount adminAuthRoutes() under auth rate-limit tier
- AppModule.kt: register AdminAuthService(get()) in Koin DI
- V2 migration: timestamps changed to BIGINT epoch-ms (consistent with Exposed long mapping)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018LvGLRUNYfVnPWs8sALLQm

* feat(admin-auth): frontend auth store, api-client, and use-auth replacement

- auth-store.ts: Zustand store for auth state (user, isLoading); not persisted —
  hydrated on every load via /admin/auth/me query
- api/auth.ts: TanStack Query hooks — useCurrentUser() (GET /me), useAdminLogin()
  (POST /login), useAdminLogout() (POST /logout)
- use-auth.ts: replaced CF JWT decoding with auth-store reads; 37-permission matrix
  mirrors AdminPermissions.kt (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE/AUDITOR/HELPDESK roles)
- api-client.ts: replaced CF-Access-Token header with credentials:'include' (httpOnly
  cookies sent automatically); 401 hook redirects to /login
- constants.ts: removed CF_COOKIE_NAME; added ROUTES.LOGIN
- types/user.ts: aligned AdminRole (ADMIN/OPERATOR/FINANCE/AUDITOR/HELPDESK) and
  AdminUser fields with backend AdminUserResponse schema

* feat(admin-auth): login page, route guard, and routeTree registration

- routes/login.tsx: ZyntaPOS branded login page with email+password form;
  react-hook-form + zod validation; show/hide password toggle; mapped server
  errors (401 invalid creds, 429 locked, 403 inactive); redirects to / on success
- routes/__root.tsx: auth guard — calls useCurrentUser() on every load to hydrate
  auth state from httpOnly cookie; redirects unauthenticated users to /login;
  renders AppShell only when authenticated; login page renders without AppShell
- routeTree.gen.ts: manually registered /login route (LoginRoute) in all type
  interfaces and rootRouteChildren map

Remove root-level shell scripts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add permission denial detection from tool_result errors and allow-list UI

Detect permission denials from CLI tool_result is_error messages (patterns:
"requires approval", "haven't granted it yet", "was blocked") by correlating
tool_call and tool_result entries via toolId. Adds PermissionDenialRow UI
with one-click "Allow" button that adds permissions to settings.local.json
via new POST /api/claude-settings/add-permission endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
